message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German Socio-Economic Panel (SOEP), Data for Years 1984-2019 (v36)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.5684/soep-core.v36","justification":"SOEP contains rich microdata on employment, income, migration background, and social participation in Germany, including respondents in the territory of the former GDR during the 1990s. It covers economic conditions, labour market status, and inequality-related indicators relevant to the requested topics and time frame."},{"title":"SOEP East Sample: Life in the New Federal States (SOEP-East)","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=2731","justification":"This specific SOEP subsample focuses on residents of the former GDR (new Länder) and tracks their labour market status, income, and living conditions after unification, covering the 1990s. It is directly relevant to employment, economic conditions, and social exclusion in the former GDR territory."},{"title":"Mikrozensus, Scientific Use Files, 1991–1999 (Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=3000","justification":"The German Microcensus SUFs for the 1990s include large-scale annual data on employment status, labour market participation, household income, and some migration indicators. They allow separate analyses for the eastern German states corresponding to the former GDR during the 1990s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 1991","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.3911","justification":"ALLBUS 1991 includes samples from both East and West Germany shortly after unification, with variables on employment status, social inequality, cultural activities, and political attitudes. East German respondents represent the former GDR population in the early 1990s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 1994","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.3912","justification":"ALLBUS 1994 provides data for East and West Germany, including employment status, income, social inequality, and participation in cultural activities. It enables comparisons and analysis of social exclusion and living conditions of former GDR residents in the mid-1990s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 1996","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.3913","justification":"ALLBUS 1996 contains variables on employment, income, social stratification, and cultural participation, with a separate East German sample. This allows analysing equality/inequality and social exclusion in the former GDR regions during the mid-1990s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 1998","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.3914","justification":"ALLBUS 1998 again covers both East and West Germany and includes questions on employment, income, social inequality, and cultural activities. East German respondents are those living in the area of the former GDR in the late 1990s."},{"title":"Population Survey on Living Conditions, Security and Satisfaction in East Germany 1990–1994","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=2403","justification":"This panel study focuses specifically on East Germany (former GDR territory) after unification and covers living conditions, employment status, income, social security, and satisfaction. It is directly aligned with employment, economic conditions, and social exclusion topics in the early 1990s."},{"title":"German Youth (DJI) Survey 1992 – East Germany Subsample","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=3103","justification":"The DJI Youth Survey includes a dedicated East German subsample in 1992 with information on education, employment status, leisure and cultural participation, and social inequality perceptions among young people in the former GDR."},{"title":"Cultural Participation and Leisure Activities in Germany 1991 (East and West)","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=2921","justification":"This dataset focuses on cultural activities and leisure participation and includes separate data for East Germany. It allows analysis of cultural participation and potential social exclusion among residents of the former GDR in the early 1990s."}]}
